items.title: SA primary school principals' association query free public school policy

children.content: A peak body for primary school principals says it is worried abolishing public school fees will see more money flow to wealthier schools, but the SA education minister says all parents were "doing it tough" and federal funding would go to addressing inequality.

thumbnail.altText: A composite image of a man and a woman looking at each other

items.title: Duo charged after police find $320,000 allegedly stolen Lego haul

children.content: A man and woman will face court after allegedly stealing hundreds of Lego sets from retail outlets across metropolitan Adelaide.

image.altText: Several boxes of Lego on pallets in a large shed

items.title: Free dental clinic changes lives in cost-of-living crisis

children.content: Demand is high for an Adelaide dental program that offers free services to patients who have experienced homelessness or who have trouble accessing conventional care.

image.altText: A woman smiles as she holds a guitar across her lap, sitting in a park

items.title: Fears for stingray populations as algal bloom moves north

children.content: Evidence of South Australia's harmful algal bloom has been detected in previously untouched parts of the Spencer Gulf days after rays were seen gathering in shallow waters.

gallery.altText: A dead stingray lying in a small puddle among rocks, with its white belly facing up

thumbnail.altText: A dead stingray lying in a small puddle among rocks, with its white belly facing up

items.title: Could nurse practitioners help ease the pressure on regional health?

children.content: Nurse practitioners are helping ease some of the burden on doctors in rural areas as services look for more options to see patients.

image.altText: A man with brown hair and a full beard smiling

items.title: Shooters deployed to curb Kangaroo Island's big little corella problem

children.content: Thermal imaging, infrared technology and crack marksmanship are deployed to battle the island's growing little corella population.

image.altText: A little corella in a tree with its wings slightly open

items.title: 'It's tragic': Hundreds to be impacted after seafood giant folds

children.content: Australia's largest wild-caught prawn operation is to close its doors after 60 years in business, impacting hundreds of workers and devastating a Queensland community.

image.altText: raptis fleets docked at the karumba wharf

items.title: MP's extra voting paper for SA upper house among electoral problems under scrutiny

children.content: As authorities commit to a review of problems faced by South Australian voters during this month's election, a former Liberal MP says he was concerned to received an extra ballot paper in the post.

image.altText: A collection of SA ballot papers including two for the Legislative Council.

items.title: Woman who appeared 'belligerent' after fatal crash sentenced

children.content: A woman who was found to be mentally incompetent when she caused a fatal multi-car crash has been released into a transitional mental health facility.

image.altText: A woman with long dark hair wearing a black tshirt sits in a room at a desk. A green chair is behind her

items.title: Concerns about light and noise from $1b outback energy project resolved

children.content: The battery project, which will provide back-up power for up to 80,000 homes in Broken Hill, will go ahead with new conditions after a dispute was resolved between the company and an outback stargazing business.

image.altText: Aerial view of highway with a few residences pictured on the ground.

items.title: Businesses report increase in abuse from customers as fuel crisis worsens

children.content: From pile-on comments on social media to roadside slurs, local businesses are reminding their communities to "be kind to the little guy" amid the fuel crisis.

image.altText: a woman stares at the camera with a houseboat in the background

items.title: Why it's a 'no-brainer' that teachers are fleeing this broken system

children.content: Victoria bills itself as the 'education state' but a growing exodus of teachers and underfunding has that tag under threat.

image.altText: A woman sits on a sofa.

items.title: Anthropic boss makes big call on Australian copyright dispute

children.content: The AI company's billionaire boss says he is not trying to convince Australia to change its mind on protecting the copyright of artists.

image.altText: A man and a woman sit on a stage at a forum with a crowd underneath.

items.title: Study shows gen Z craves in-person connection more than other generations

children.content: Gen Z is embracing retro technology like iPods, vinyl and Polaroid cameras and craves in-person connection more than any other generation, according to a Live Nation study.

image.altText: A young woman with long blonde hair wearing a pink and black cap, pink shirt, rolling her eyes

items.title: Arnold carried in jubilant scenes after Iraq qualifies for World Cup

children.content: Former Socceroos coach Graham Arnold has guided Iraq to their first FIFA World Cup appearance in 40 years with a playoff win over Bolivia.

thumbnail.altText: Iraq's coach Graham Arnold carried by players after qualifying for FIFA World Cup, massive crowd waiving flags

items.title: Inside Queensland's first recovery village for eating disorder survivors

children.content: A couple whose daughters survived eating disorders is opening a first-of-its-kind recovery village to help survivors ease back into their normal lives without relapsing.

image.altText: picture of Mark and Gaye Forbes smiling

items.title: Former Sydney University lecturer seen in Iran video about Hormuz Strait

children.content: An Australian academic has appeared in an Iranian propaganda video supporting a toll system imposed by the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps on foreign merchant shipping.

image.altText: A man gives the peace sign with his hand.

items.title: Dezi Freeman investigation to probe January bushfires 1km from hiding spot

children.content: Victoria Police have officially confirmed that the person shot dead by officers at a property in Victoria's north-east on Monday morning was fugitive Dezi Freeman.

image.altText: Dezi Freeman pictured outside a court in Wangaratta.

items.title: Dire future of superb fairy-wren could mean catastrophe for other species

children.content: The researchers said the superb fairy-wren would likely go extinct within 30-40 years unless action was taken to stop greenhouse gas emissions causing climate change.

image.altText: A small blue-headed wren sitting in a tree.

items.title: How this Aussie went from TAFE to the head of Sony character animation

children.content: Daniel Pozo has had a hand in Sony hits like Spiderman: Across the Spider-Verse and Hotel Transylvania, but GOAT is his first project in the driver's seat.

items.title: Lawsuit filed against Taylor Swift over The Life of a Showgirl album

image.altText: Taylor Swift poses in a body of water in a glittering silver corset

items.title: Bluey's lead composer on helping kids fall in love with music

children.content: A new orchestral Bluey album is out. Lead composer Joff Bush talks about classical music, storytelling and inspiring kids to love music.

image.altText: A man in a colourful shirt plays the melodica

items.title: National addresses are rare and historic. Now, Albanese has made another

children.content: Pandemics, wars, and landmark moments have been immortalised in addresses to the nation. Anthony Albanese has become one of the few prime ministers to employ the form.

items.title: Crucial exhibits unveiled to jury in Irma Palasics cold case trial

children.content: A milk container and water jug carrying DNA evidence were revealed to jurors as a decades-old murder trial reached its final stage.

image.altText: A fridge with many containers in it.

items.title: Australia's solar panel waste issue is growing in size year-on-year

children.content: Australians are expected to throw away 90,000 tonnes of solar panel waste each year by 2030, a federal government hearing has been told.

image.altText: Four rows of dark solar panels on a silver roof.

items.title: New mine owner claims he's faced threats over removing bikie shareholders

children.content: The Tasmanian government has taken steps to revoke the operating licence of one of the state's biggest mining operations, after being warned it was allegedly used as a "vehicle for money laundering" and former owners were associated with underworld figures.

items.title: NBL owner prepared to walk away from major land purchase

items.title: Cultural compensation replaces fine in 'rare' illegal logging ruling

children.content: Traditional owners say a $450,000 fine will not replace the six centuries-old giant trees illegally felled by NSW Forestry Corporation.

items.title: 'We are an expert': How Lebanon's airport keeps running despite Israeli strikes

children.content: Beirut-Rafic Hariri Airport, Lebanon's gateway to the world, is still operating even as Israel bombards its surroundings.

items.title: More than 290,000 visits to Daniel's Law website in first three months

children.content: Queensland's first public child sex offender register, listing reportable offenders who have failed to comply with their reporting obligations or whose whereabouts are unknown, has been praised for protecting children.

items.title: These old sugarcane farms could be turned into rainforest again

children.content: A conservation group's plans to replant species from the World Heritage-listed Daintree Rainforest on land used for growing sugar have not won everybody over.

items.title: Spurs fans protest over new manager as post-Postecoglou woes deepen

children.content: Tottenham fan groups voice opposition to newly appointed manager Roberto De Zerbi over his previous public support for Mason Greenwood, a player who faced charges of attempted rape and assault.

image.altText: Roberto De Zerbi sits on the bench and looks to his left

items.title: 'Nothing's out here' Vodafone ad angers rural Australians

children.content: A new Vodafone campaign has regional Australians seething after the telecommunications giant claimed to focus its network infrastructure on where "98 whatever per cent of you actually live".

items.title: Council monitor appointed after character reference saga embroils mayor
